name: Beef Stew with Cuminseed
recipeIngredient: 1 teaspoon Salt, plus extra to taste
3 pounds Beef stew meat, cut into 1-inch cubes
recipeInstructions:
1. In a shallow bowl, stir together the flour, thyme, salt, and pepper.
2. Coat the beef cubes well in the flour mixture and shake off excess. Transfer to a plate.
3. Heat olive oil in a heavy kettle. Add beef cubes in batches and brown on all sides. Drain on paper towels.
4. When all meat is browned, discard excess oil but do not wash the kettle. Add wine, beef stock, and crushed tomatoes; bring to a boil, stirring and scraping up browned bits.
5. Return beef to the kettle, then add cuminseed, chili powder, and bay leaf. Season with salt and pepper.
6. Cover kettle and place on middle rack of oven. Bake at 350°F for 1 1/2 hours, stirring occasionally and adjusting oven temperature to maintain a steady simmer.
7. Meanwhile, boil water and cut an "X" in the root end of each pearl onion. Drop into boiling water for 1 minute, then into cold water. Drain and peel.
8. After 1 hour in the oven, stir in onions. Continue cooking uncovered until beef is tender and stew thickens to desired consistency. Stir in garlic, parsley, and olives. Cook uncovered until reduced and thickened.
9. Transfer to serving bowl, garnish with parsley, and serve immediately.
